message:
✨ Add educational intro modal to /acmm + grammar fix (#8360)

First-time visitors to /acmm now see a sticky modal that explains the
AI Codebase Maturity Model: what it is, the 5 levels, the 4 source
frameworks (with badges), what they can do on the page, and a link to
the underlying paper. Replaces the lonely spinning loader.

Modal does not close on backdrop click or Escape so users have time to
read; only the explicit Close button (or X) dismisses it. A "Don't
show this again" checkbox persists to localStorage so returning users
skip it automatically.

Also fixes the "You are a Executor" / "You are a Analyst" grammar bug
on the Your Role card — uses "an" when the role starts with a vowel.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,230 @@ +/** + * ACMM Intro Modal + * + * Educational modal shown on first visit to /acmm. Explains what the + * AI Codebase Maturity Model is, the 5 levels, the 4 source frameworks, + * and links to the underlying paper. Modal is "sticky" — does not close + * on backdrop click or Escape so users have time to read; only the + * explicit Close button (or the X in the header) dismisses it. + * + * Persists a "don't show again" preference in localStorage so returning + * users skip the modal automatically. The preference can always be + * reset by clearing the localStorage key. + */ + +import { useState, useEffect } from 'react' +import { BarChart3, ExternalLink, BookOpen, Layers, Wrench, GitBranch } from 'lucide-react' +import { BaseModal } from '../../lib/modals' + +const STORAGE_KEY = 'kc-acmm-intro-dismissed' +const PAPER_URL = 'https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.09388' + +export function isACMMIntroDismissed(): boolean { + try { + return localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY) === '1' + } catch { + return false + } +} + +function dismissACMMIntro() { + try { + localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, '1') + } catch { + // localStorage unavailable — silently ignore + } +} + +interface ACMMIntroModalProps { + isOpen: boolean + onClose: () => void +} + +export function ACMMIntroModal({ isOpen, onClose }: ACMMIntroModalProps) { + const [dontShowAgain, setDontShowAgain] = useState(false) + + function handleClose() { + if (dontShowAgain) { + dismissACMMIntro() + } + onClose() + } + + return ( + + + + +
+ {/* What is ACMM */} +
+
+ +

What is ACMM?

+
+

+ The AI Codebase Maturity Model is a 5-level framework that scores how + ready your repository is for AI-assisted engineering. It looks for + concrete, detectable signals — instruction files, measurement + workflows, feedback loops, gating policies — and scores from{' '} + L1 Assisted (an AI + suggests completions) up to{' '} + L5 Self-Sustaining{' '} + (the codebase proposes, triages, and gates its own work). +

+

+ The dashboard scans the repo's file structure, workflows, and + configuration to detect these signals. No source code is uploaded; + only public file paths are read. +

+
+ + {/* The 5 levels */} +
+
+ +

The 5 levels

+
+
+
+ L1 + Assisted + AI suggests completions, no persistent rules +
+
+ L2 + Instructed + Judgment encoded in CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / Copilot instructions +
+
+ L3 + Measured + Metrics instrument the AI loop itself +
+
+ L4 + Adaptive + Metrics feed back into instructions and gating thresholds +
+
+ L5 + Self-Sustaining + Codebase proposes, triages, and gates its own work +
+
+
+ + {/* Source frameworks */} +
+
+ +

Source frameworks

+
+

+ ACMM aggregates criteria from four open-source frameworks. Each + criterion in the inventory is tagged with its source so you can + follow the citation trail back to the upstream definition. +

+
    +
  • + ACMM{' '} + — the 5-level model itself +
    • +
  • + Fullsend{' '} + — readiness + autonomy criteria (test coverage, CI/CD, auto-merge policy) +
    • +
  • + AEF{' '} + — Agentic Engineering Framework: governance criteria (task traceability, structural gates) +
    • +
  • + Reflect{' '} + — Claude Reflect: self-tuning criteria (correction capture, CLAUDE.md auto-sync) +
    • +
+
+ + {/* What you can do here */} +
+
+ +

What you can do here

+
+
    +
  • Type any owner/repo in the picker above to scan it.
    • +
  • See your role and the next transition trigger on the Your Role card.
    • +
  • Browse all detected vs missing criteria on the Feedback Loops Inventory card.
    • +
  • For any missing criterion, click Ask agent for help to launch an agent that adds it.
    • +
  • Generate a shields.io badge for your README from the picker — it updates as your score changes.
    • +
+
+ + {/* Paper link */} +
+ + + Read the paper on arXiv (2604.09388) + +
+
+ + + +
+ + +
+ + + ) +} + +/** + * Hook that controls intro modal visibility for the /acmm page. + * Opens on mount unless the user previously dismissed with "don't show again". + */ +export function useACMMIntroModal() { + const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = useState(false) + + useEffect(() => { + if (!isACMMIntroDismissed()) { + setIsOpen(true) + } + }, []) + + return { + isOpen, + onClose: () => setIsOpen(false), + } +}
